You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 07297749).

## Issue to Resolve

Wrap annotations before class constructor (ktlint_official)
Annotations before the class constructor should be wrapped in same way as other annotations when code style `ktlint_official` is used.

Example:
```
data class Foo @Bar1 @Bar2("bar") @Bar3 @Bar4 constructor(private val foobar: Int) {
    fun foo(): String = "foo"
}
```
should be formatted as
```
data class Foo
    @Bar1
    @Bar2("bar")
    @Bar3
    @Bar4
    constructor(private val foobar: Int) {
    fun foo(): String = "foo"
}
```
Annotations on type projections should be wrapped in same way as other annotations
Given a type argument list in which one or more of the type projects are annotation than the same wrapping rules should be applies as with other annotations.

Exampe:
```
val fooBar: FooBar<String, @Foo String, @Foo @Bar String, @Bar("bar") @Foo String> = FooBar()
```
should be formatted as:
```
val fooBar: FooBar<
    String,
    @Foo String,
    @Foo @Bar
    String,
    @Bar("bar")
    @Foo
    String
    > = FooBar()
```

In case the type argument list only contains type projections without or with a single annotation without parameter than the annotation does not need to be wrapped. So code below will not be changed:
```
val foo1: List<@Foo String> = emptyList()
val foo2 = emptyList<@Foo String>()
val foo3: Map<@Foo String, @Foo String> = emptyMap()
val foo4 = emptyMap<@Foo String, @Foo String>()
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/cli/api/Baseline.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/AnnotationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/TypeArgumentListSpacingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 46→/**
 47→ * Ensures that annotation are wrapped to separate lines.
 48→ *
 49→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#annotation-formatting
 50→ *
 51→ * 1) Place annotations on separate lines before the declaration to which they are attached, and with the same indentation:
 52→ *
 53→ *    ```
 54→ *    @Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
 55→ *    annotation class JsonExclude
 56→ *    ```
 57→ *
 58→ * 2) Annotations without arguments may be placed on the same line:
 59→ *
 60→ *    ```
 61→ *    @JsonExclude @JvmField
 62→ *    var x: String
 63→ *    ```
 64→ *
 65→ * 3) A single annotation without arguments may be placed on the same line as the corresponding declaration:
 66→ *
 67→ *    ```
 68→ *    @Test fun foo() { /*...*/ }
 69→ *    ```
 70→ *
 71→ * 4) File annotations are placed after the file comment (if any), before the package statement, and are separated from package with a blank
 72→ *    line (to emphasize the fact that they target the file and not the package).
 73→ *
 74→ * @see [AnnotationSpacingRule] for white space rules.
 75→ */
 76→public class AnnotationRule :
 77→    StandardRule(
 78→        id = "annotation",
 79→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 80→            setOf(
 81→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 82→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 83→            ),
 84→        visitorModifiers =
 85→            setOf(
 86→                RunAfterRule(
 87→                    ruleId = ENUM_WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 88→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 89→                ),
 90→            ),
 91→    ) {
 92→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 93→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 94→
 95→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 96→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 97→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 98→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 99→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
100→        )
101→    }
102→
103→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
104→        node: ASTNode,
105→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
106→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
107→    ) {
108→        when (node.elementType) {
109→            FILE_ANNOTATION_LIST -> {
110→                visitFileAnnotationList(node, emit, autoCorrect)
111→            }
112→            ANNOTATION -> {
113→                // Annotation array
114→                //     @[...]
115→                visitAnnotation(node, emit, autoCorrect)
116→            }
117→            ANNOTATION_ENTRY -> {
118→                visitAnnotationEntry(node, emit, autoCorrect)
119→            }
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun visitAnnotationEntry(
124→        node: ASTNode,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
129→
130→        if (node.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() &&
131→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
132→            node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
133→            node.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation()
134→        ) {
135→            // Disallow:
136→            //     @Foo class Bar
137→            // Allow function parameters and arguments to have annotation on same line as identifier:
138→            //     fun @receiver:Bar String.foo() {}
139→            //     fun foo(
140→            //         @Bar("bar") bar,
141→            //         @Bar("bar") "42",
142→            //     ) {}
143→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
144→        }
145→
146→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
147→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
148→                // Code below is disallowed
149→                //   @Foo1
150→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
151→                emit(
152→                    node.startOffset,
153→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
154→                    true,
155→                )
156→                if (autoCorrect) {
157→                    node
158→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
159→                        .nextLeaf()
160→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
161→                }
162→            }
163→
164→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
165→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
166→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
167→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
168→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
169→            ) {
170→                // Code below is disallowed
171→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
172→                // But following is allowed:
173→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
174→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
175→                emit(
176→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
177→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
178→                    true,
179→                )
180→                if (autoCorrect) {
181→                    node
182→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
183→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
184→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
185→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
186→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
187→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
188→                                },
189→                        )
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
194→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
195→                ?.let { startOfList ->
196→                    // Code below is disallowed
197→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
198→                    // But following is allowed:
199→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
200→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
201→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
202→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
203→                    ) {
204→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
205→                        if (autoCorrect) {
206→                            startOfList
207→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
208→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
209→                                )
210→                        }
211→                    }
212→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
213→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
214→                    ) {
215→                        node
216→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
217→                            .treeParent
218→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
219→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
220→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
221→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
222→                                if (autoCorrect) {
223→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
224→                                }
225→                            }
226→                    }
227→                }
228→        }
229→
230→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
231→            // Code below is disallowed
232→            //   @Foo1
233→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
234→            //   fun foo() {}
235→            emit(
236→                node.startOffset,
237→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
238→                true,
239→            )
240→            if (autoCorrect) {
241→                node
242→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
243→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
244→            }
245→        }
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
249→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
250→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
251→            ?.treeParent
252→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
253→            ?.treeParent
254→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
255→            ?.treeParent
256→
257→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
258→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
259→        return siblings(forward = false)
260→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
261→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
262→    }
263→
264→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
265→        node: ASTNode,
266→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
267→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
268→    ) {
269→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
270→            emit(
271→                node.startOffset,
272→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
273→                true,
274→            )
275→            if (autoCorrect) {
276→                node
277→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
278→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
279→            }
280→        }
281→
282→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
283→            emit(
284→                node.startOffset,
285→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
286→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
287→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
288→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
289→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
290→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
291→            )
292→            if (autoCorrect) {
293→                node
294→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
295→                    .nextLeaf()
296→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
297→                    ?.let {
298→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
299→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
300→                        } else {
301→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
302→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
303→                            )
304→                        }
305→                    }
306→            }
307→        }
308→    }
309→
310→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
311→
312→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
313→        psi
314→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
315→            ?.useSiteTarget
316→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
317→
318→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
319→
320→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
321→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
322→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
323→
324→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
325→        this ==
326→            treeParent
327→                .children()
328→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
329→
330→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
331→        this ==
332→            treeParent
333→                .children()
334→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
335→
336→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
337→        siblings(forward = false)
338→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
339→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
340→
341→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
342→        siblings()
343→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
344→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
345→
346→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
347→        siblings(forward = false)
348→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
349→
350→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
351→        siblings(forward = false)
352→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
353→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
354→
355→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
356→
357→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
358→        siblings(forward = true)
359→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
360→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
363→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
364→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
365→            .leaves(forward = true)
366→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
367→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
371→        val astNode =
372→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
373→                treeParent
374→            } else {
375→                this
376→            }
377→        return checkNotNull(
378→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
379→        )
380→    }
381→
382→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
383→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
384→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
385→        } else {
386→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
387→        }
388→
389→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
390→        node: ASTNode,
391→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
392→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
393→    ) {
394→        node
395→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
396→            .nextCodeLeaf()
397→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
398→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
399→
400→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
401→                    emit(
402→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
403→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
404→                        true,
405→                    )
406→                    if (autoCorrect) {
407→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
408→                    }
409→                }
410→            }
411→    }
412→
413→    private fun visitAnnotation(
414→        node: ASTNode,
415→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
416→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
417→    ) {
418→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
419→
420→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
421→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
422→        ) {
423→            emit(
424→                node.startOffset,
425→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
426→                true,
427→            )
428→            if (autoCorrect) {
429→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
430→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
431→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
432→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
433→                }
434→            }
435→        }
436→    }
437→
438→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
439→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
440→            modifierListRoot
441→                .treeParent
442→                .treePrev
443→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
444→        val indentWithoutNewline =
445→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
446→                ?.text
447→                .orEmpty()
448→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
449→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
450→    }
451→}
452→
453→public val ANNOTATION_RULE_ID: RuleId = AnnotationRule().ruleId
454→

 122→public class IndentationRule :
 123→    StandardRule(
 124→        id = "indent",
 125→        visitorModifiers =
 126→            setOf(
 127→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 128→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 129→                    ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
 130→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 131→                ),
 132→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 133→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 134→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 135→                ),
 136→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 137→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 138→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 139→                ),
 140→            ),
 141→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 142→            setOf(
 143→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 144→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 145→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 146→            ),
 147→    ) {
 148→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 149→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 150→
 151→    private var line = 1
 152→
 153→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 154→
 155→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 156→
 157→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 158→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 159→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 160→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 161→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 162→        )
 163→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 164→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 165→        }
 166→    }
 167→
 168→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 169→        node: ASTNode,
 170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 172→    ) {
 173→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 174→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 175→            node
 176→                .nextLeaf()
 177→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 178→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 179→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 180→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 181→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 182→                    }
 183→                }
 184→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 185→        }
 186→
 187→        when {
 188→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 189→                line++
 190→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 191→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 192→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 193→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 194→                    )
 195→                }
 196→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 197→            }
 198→
 199→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 200→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 201→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 202→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
 203→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 204→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 205→                startIndentContext(
 206→                    fromAstNode = node,
 207→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 208→                )
 209→
 210→            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 211→                visitValueArgument(node)
 212→
 213→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 214→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 215→
 216→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 217→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 218→                startIndentContext(node)
 219→
 220→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 221→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 222→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 223→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 224→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 225→                startIndentContext(node)
 226→
 227→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 228→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 229→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 230→                startIndentContext(
 231→                    fromAstNode = node,
 232→                    childIndent = "",
 233→                )
 234→
 235→            node.elementType == IF ->
 236→                visitIf(node)
 237→
 238→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 239→                visitLbrace(node)
 240→
 241→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 242→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 243→                startIndentContext(
 244→                    fromAstNode = node,
 245→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 246→                )
 247→
 248→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 249→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 250→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 251→
 252→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 253→                visitValueParameter(node)
 254→
 255→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 256→                visitFun(node)
 257→
 258→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 259→                visitClass(node)
 260→
 261→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 262→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 263→
 264→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 265→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 266→
 267→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 268→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 269→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 270→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 271→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 272→                ) {
 273→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 274→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 275→                    //    .bar[0] {
 276→                    //        "foobar"
 277→                    //    }
 278→                    startIndentContext(
 279→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 280→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 281→                    )
 282→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 283→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 284→                } else {
 285→                    startIndentContext(node)
 286→                }
 287→            }
 288→
 289→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 290→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 291→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 292→
 293→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 294→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 295→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 296→
 297→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 298→                visitWhen(node)
 299→
 300→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 301→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 302→
 303→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 304→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 305→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 306→
 307→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 308→                visitKdoc(node)
 309→
 310→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 311→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 312→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 313→
 314→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 315→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 316→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 317→
 318→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 319→                visitLBracket(node)
 320→
 321→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 322→                visitNullableType(node)
 323→
 324→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 325→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 326→
 327→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 328→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 329→
 330→            else -> {
 331→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 332→            }
 333→        }
 334→    }

 336→    private fun visitValueArgument(node: ASTNode) {
 337→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 338→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 339→            //     val foo = foo(
 340→            //         parameterName =
 341→            //             "The quick brown fox "
 342→            //                .plus("jumps ")
 343→            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 344→            //     )
 345→            startIndentContext(
 346→                fromAstNode = node,
 347→                lastChildIndent = "",
 348→            )
 349→        }
 350→    }

 352→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 353→        node
 354→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 355→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 356→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 357→                val nextToAstNode =
 358→                    startIndentContext(
 359→                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 360→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 361→
 362→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 363→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 364→                    startIndentContext(
 365→                        fromAstNode = node,
 366→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 367→                        childIndent = "",
 368→                    )
 369→                }
 370→            }
 371→    }

 373→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 374→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 375→        node
 376→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 377→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 378→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 379→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 380→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 381→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 382→            }
 383→
 384→        node
 385→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 386→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 387→            ?.nextLeaf()
 388→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 389→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 390→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 391→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 392→                    childIndent = "",
 393→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 394→            }
 395→        node
 396→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 397→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 398→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 399→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 400→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 401→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 402→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 403→            }
 404→        startIndentContext(
 405→            fromAstNode = node,
 406→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 407→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 408→        )
 409→    }

 411→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 412→        // Outer indent context
 413→        val rbrace =
 414→            requireNotNull(
 415→                node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 416→            ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 417→        startIndentContext(
 418→            fromAstNode = node,
 419→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 420→            firstChildIndent = "",
 421→            lastChildIndent = "",
 422→        )
 423→
 424→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 425→        node
 426→            .treeParent
 427→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 428→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 429→            ?.let { arrow ->
 430→                startIndentContext(
 431→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 432→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 433→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 434→                )
 435→                startIndentContext(
 436→                    fromAstNode = node,
 437→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 438→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 439→                )
 440→            }
 441→    }
 442→
 443→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 444→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 445→            // val fieldExample =
 446→            //      LongNameClass {
 447→            //              paramA,
 448→            //              paramB,
 449→            //              paramC ->
 450→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 451→            //      }
 452→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 453→        } else {
 454→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 455→            // val fieldExample =
 456→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 457→            //                     paramB,
 458→            //                     paramC ->
 459→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 460→            //     }
 461→            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 462→                ?.let { callExpression ->
 463→                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 464→                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 465→                            " { "
 466→                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 467→                }
 468→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 469→        }
 470→
 471→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 472→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 473→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 474→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 475→
 476→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 477→        startIndentContext(
 478→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 479→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 480→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 481→            childIndent = "",
 482→        )
 483→    }
 484→
 485→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 486→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 487→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 488→        node
 489→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 490→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 491→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 492→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 493→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 494→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 495→            }
 496→
 497→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official) {
 498→            // Deviate from standard IntelliJ IDEA formatting to allow formatting below:
 499→            //     fun process(
 500→            //         aVariableWithAVeryLongName:
 501→            //             TypeWithAVeryLongNameThatDoesNotFitOnSameLineAsTheVariableName
 502→            //     ): List<Output>
 503→            node
 504→                .findChildByType(COLON)
 505→                ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 506→                    nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 507→                        fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 508→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 509→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 510→                }
 511→        }
 512→
 513→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 514→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 515→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&

 529→        }

 617→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {

 624→            startIndentContext(

 626→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 627→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 628→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,

 632→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS

 896→    ) {

 902→                "Remove indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for ${indentContext.fromASTNode.elementType}: " +
 903→                    "${indentContext.nodes}"

 906→                .removeLast()
 907→                .also {
 908→                    LOGGER.trace {

 912→                        "Last indent context with level ($nodeIndentLevel, $childIndentLevel) for " +

 926→    private fun visitNewLineIndentation(
 927→        node: ASTNode,
 928→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 929→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 930→    ) {
 931→        if (node.ignoreIndent()) {
 932→            return
 933→        }
 934→
 935→        val normalizedNodeIndent = node.normalizedIndent(emit)
 936→        val expectedIndentation = node.expectedIndent()
 937→        val text = node.text
 938→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
 939→        if (nodeIndent != normalizedNodeIndent || normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 940→            if (normalizedNodeIndent != expectedIndentation) {
 941→                emit(
 942→                    node.startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
 943→                    "Unexpected indentation (${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) (should be ${expectedIndentation.length})",
 944→                    true,
 945→                )
 946→            } else {
 947→                // Indentation was at correct level but contained invalid indent characters. This violation has already
 948→                // been emitted.
 949→            }
 950→            LOGGER.trace {
 951→                "Line $line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "changed indentation to ${expectedIndentation.length} " +
 952→                    "(from ${normalizedNodeIndent.length}) for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}"
 953→            }
 954→            if (autoCorrect) {
 955→                (node as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(
 956→                    text.substringBeforeLast("\n") + "\n" + expectedIndentation,
 957→                )
 958→            }
 959→        } else {
 960→            node.processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 961→        }
 962→    }

 964→    private fun ASTNode.ignoreIndent(): Boolean {
 965→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 966→        if (text.endsWith("\n") && nextLeaf.isStartOfRawStringLiteral()) {
 967→            processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 968→            return true // raw strings (""") are allowed at column 0
 969→        }
 970→
 971→        nextLeaf
 972→            ?.parent(strict = false) { it.psi is PsiComment }
 973→            ?.let { comment ->
 974→                if (text.endsWith("\n")) {
 975→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 976→                    return true // comments are allowed at column 0
 977→                }
 978→                if (comment.textContains('\n') && comment.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 979→                    // FIXME: while we cannot assume any kind of layout inside a block comment,
 980→                    // `/*` and `*/` can still be indented
 981→                    processedButNoIndentationChangedNeeded()
 982→                    return true
 983→                }
 984→            }
 985→        return false
 986→    }

 993→    private fun ASTNode.expectedIndent(): String {
 994→        val lastIndexContext = indentContextStack.peekLast()
 995→        val nextLeaf = nextLeaf()
 996→        val adjustedChildIndent =
 997→            when {
 998→                this == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ||
 999→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.fromASTNode.firstChildLeafOrSelf() ->
1000→                    lastIndexContext.firstChildIndent
1001→
1002→                this == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ||
1003→                    nextLeaf == lastIndexContext.toASTNode ->
1004→                    lastIndexContext.lastChildIndent
1005→
1006→                else -> lastIndexContext.childIndent
1007→            }
1008→        return lastIndexContext.nodeIndent + adjustedChildIndent
1009→    }

1011→    private fun ASTNode.normalizedIndent(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit): String {
1012→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1013→        return when (indentConfig.indentStyle) {
1014→            SPACE -> {
1015→                if ('\t' in nodeIndent) {
1016→                    emit(
1017→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1018→                        "Unexpected tab character(s)",
1019→                        true,
1020→                    )
1021→                    indentConfig.toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndent)
1022→                } else {
1023→                    nodeIndent
1024→                }
1025→            }
1026→
1027→            TAB -> {
1028→                val acceptableTrailingSpaces = acceptableTrailingSpaces()
1029→                val nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces = nodeIndent.removeSuffix(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1030→                if (' ' in nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces) {
1031→                    emit(
1032→                        startOffset + text.length - nodeIndent.length,
1033→                        "Unexpected space character(s)",
1034→                        true,
1035→                    )
1036→                    indentConfig
1037→                        .toNormalizedIndent(nodeIndentWithoutAcceptableTrailingSpaces)
1038→                        .plus(acceptableTrailingSpaces)
1039→                } else {
1040→                    nodeIndent
1041→                }
1042→            }
1043→        }
1044→    }

1057→    private fun ASTNode.acceptableTrailingSpaces(): String {
1058→        require(elementType == WHITE_SPACE)
1059→        val acceptableTrailingSpaces =
1060→            when (nextLeaf()?.elementType) {
1061→                KDOC_LEADING_ASTERISK, KDOC_END -> {
1062→                    // The indentation of a KDoc comment contains a space as the last character regardless of the indentation
1063→                    // style (tabs or spaces) except for the starting line of the KDoc comment
1064→                    KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1065→                }
1066→
1067→                TYPE_CONSTRAINT -> {
1068→                    // 6 spaces (length of "where" keyword plus a separator space) to indent type constraints as below:
1069→                    //    where A1 : RecyclerView.Adapter<V1>,
1070→                    //               A1 : ComposableAdapter.ViewTypeProvider,
1071→                    TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT
1072→                }
1073→
1074→                else -> ""
1075→            }
1076→        val nodeIndent = text.substringAfterLast("\n")
1077→        return if (nodeIndent.endsWith(acceptableTrailingSpaces)) {
1078→            return acceptableTrailingSpaces
1079→        } else {
1080→            ""
1081→        }
1082→    }

1100→    private data class IndentContext(
1101→        /**
1102→         * The node on which the indent context starts.
1103→         */
1104→        val fromASTNode: ASTNode,
1105→        /**
1106→         * The node at which the indent context ends. If null, then the context ends at the last child leaf of the node
1107→         * on which the indent context starts.
1108→         */
1109→        val toASTNode: ASTNode = fromASTNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
1110→        /**
1111→         * Cumulative indentation for the node. Normally this should be equal to a multiple of the
1112→         * 'indentConfig.indentStyle' to ensure a consistent indentation style.
1113→         */
1114→        val nodeIndent: String,
1115→        /**
1116→         * Additional indentation for first child node. Normally this should be equal to the 'indentConfig.indent' to
1117→         * ensure a consistent indentation style. In very limited cases when the default indentation is set to 'tab' it
1118→         * is still needed to adjust the last indentation using spaces.
1119→         */

1320→                .filterNot { it.endsWith("\"\"\"") }

1323→        val distinctIndentCharacters =
1324→            nonBlankLines

1346→    /**

 15→/**
 16→ * Lints and formats the spacing before and after the angle brackets of a type argument list.
 17→ */
 18→public class TypeArgumentListSpacingRule :
 19→    StandardRule("type-argument-list-spacing"),
 20→    Rule.Experimental {
 21→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 22→        node: ASTNode,
 23→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 24→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 25→    ) {
 26→        when (node.elementType) {
 27→            ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> {
 28→                visitFunctionDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 29→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 30→            }
 31→            ElementType.SUPER_TYPE_LIST, ElementType.SUPER_EXPRESSION ->
 32→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 33→        }
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    private fun visitFunctionDeclaration(
 37→        node: ASTNode,
 38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40→    ) {
 41→        // No whitespace expected before type argument list of function call
 42→        //    val list = listOf <String>()
 43→        node
 44→            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 45→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 46→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 47→
 48→        // No whitespace expected after type argument list of function call
 49→        //    val list = listOf<String> ()
 50→        node
 51→            .takeUnless {
 52→                // unless it is part of a type reference:
 53→                //    fun foo(): List<Foo> { ... }
 54→                //    var bar: List<Bar> = emptyList()
 55→                it.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType(ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE)
 56→            }
 57→            ?.takeUnless {
 58→                // unless it is part of a call expression followed by lambda:
 59→                //    bar<Foo> { ... }
 60→                it.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda()
 61→            }
 62→            ?.lastChildNode
 63→            ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 64→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 65→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    private fun visitInsideTypeArgumentList(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        // No whitespace expected after opening angle bracket of type argument list
 74→        //    val list = listOf< String>()
 75→        node
 76→            .findChildByType(ElementType.LT)
 77→            ?.nextSibling()
 78→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 79→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 80→
 81→        // No whitespace expected before closing angle bracket of type argument list
 82→        //    val list = listOf<String >()
 83→        node
 84→            .findChildByType(ElementType.GT)
 85→            ?.prevSibling()
 86→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 87→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    private fun noWhitespaceExpected(
 91→        node: ASTNode,
 92→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→    ) {
 95→        if (node.text != "") {
 96→            emit(
 97→                node.startOffset,
 98→                "No whitespace expected at this position",
 99→                true,
100→            )
101→            if (autoCorrect) {
102→                node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
103→            }
104→        }
105→    }
106→}
107→
108→private fun ASTNode.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda(): Boolean =
109→    findCompositeParentElementOfType(ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION)
110→        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION }
111→        ?.findChildByType(ElementType.LAMBDA_ARGUMENT)
112→        .let { it != null }
113→
114→public val TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = TypeArgumentListSpacingRule().ruleId

CHANGELOG.md:41:The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.

 15→/**
 16→ * Lints and formats the spacing before and after the angle brackets of a type argument list.
 17→ */
 18→public class TypeArgumentListSpacingRule :
 19→    StandardRule("type-argument-list-spacing"),
 20→    Rule.Experimental {
 21→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 22→        node: ASTNode,
 23→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 24→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 25→    ) {
 26→        when (node.elementType) {
 27→            ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> {
 28→                visitFunctionDeclaration(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 29→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 30→            }
 31→            ElementType.SUPER_TYPE_LIST, ElementType.SUPER_EXPRESSION ->
 32→                visitInsideTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 33→        }
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    private fun visitFunctionDeclaration(
 37→        node: ASTNode,
 38→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 39→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 40→    ) {
 41→        // No whitespace expected before type argument list of function call
 42→        //    val list = listOf <String>()
 43→        node
 44→            .prevLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 45→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 46→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 47→
 48→        // No whitespace expected after type argument list of function call
 49→        //    val list = listOf<String> ()
 50→        node
 51→            .takeUnless {
 52→                // unless it is part of a type reference:
 53→                //    fun foo(): List<Foo> { ... }
 54→                //    var bar: List<Bar> = emptyList()
 55→                it.isPartOfCompositeElementOfType(ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE)
 56→            }
 57→            ?.takeUnless {
 58→                // unless it is part of a call expression followed by lambda:
 59→                //    bar<Foo> { ... }
 60→                it.isPartOfCallExpressionFollowedByLambda()
 61→            }
 62→            ?.lastChildNode
 63→            ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
 64→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 65→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    private fun visitInsideTypeArgumentList(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        // No whitespace expected after opening angle bracket of type argument list
 74→        //    val list = listOf< String>()
 75→        node
 76→            .findChildByType(ElementType.LT)
 77→            ?.nextSibling()
 78→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 79→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 80→
 81→        // No whitespace expected before closing angle bracket of type argument list
 82→        //    val list = listOf<String >()
 83→        node
 84→            .findChildByType(ElementType.GT)
 85→            ?.prevSibling()
 86→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE }
 87→            ?.let { noWhitespaceExpected(it, autoCorrect, emit) }
 88→    }

 90→    private fun noWhitespaceExpected(
 91→        node: ASTNode,
 92→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→    ) {
 95→        if (node.text != "") {
 96→            emit(
 97→                node.startOffset,
 98→                "No whitespace expected at this position",
 99→                true,
100→            )
101→            if (autoCorrect) {
102→                node.treeParent.removeChild(node)
103→            }
104→        }
105→    }
106→}

111→        ?.findChildByType(ElementType.LAMBDA_ARGUMENT)
112→        .let { it != null }

142→            //     ) {}
143→            checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
144→        }
145→
146→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()) {
147→            if (node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
148→                // Code below is disallowed
149→                //   @Foo1
150→                //   @Foo2 fun foo() {}
151→                emit(
152→                    node.startOffset,
153→                    "Annotation must be placed on a separate line when it is preceded by another annotation on a separate line",
154→                    true,
155→                )
156→                if (autoCorrect) {
157→                    node
158→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
159→                        .nextLeaf()
160→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
161→                }
162→            }
163→
164→            if (node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
165→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_PARAMETER &&
166→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != VALUE_ARGUMENT &&
167→                node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
168→                node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
169→            ) {
170→                // Code below is disallowed
171→                //   @Foo1 @Foo2 fun foo() {}
172→                // But following is allowed:
173→                //   @[Foo1 Foo2] fun foo() {}
174→                //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
175→                emit(
176→                    node.findAnnotatedConstruct().startOffset,
177→                    "Multiple annotations should not be placed on the same line as the annotated construct",
178→                    true,
179→                )
180→                if (autoCorrect) {
181→                    node
182→                        .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
183→                        .nextCodeLeaf()
184→                        ?.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
185→                            getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)
186→                                .applyIf(node.typeProjectionOrNull() != null) {
187→                                    plus(indentConfig.indent)
188→                                },
189→                        )
190→                }
191→            }
192→
193→            node.typeProjectionOrNull()
194→                ?.prevCodeLeaf()
195→                ?.let { startOfList ->
196→                    // Code below is disallowed
197→                    //   var foo: List<@Foo1 @Foo2 String>
198→                    // But following is allowed:
199→                    //   var foo: List<@[Foo1 Foo2] String>
200→                    //   fun foo(@Bar1 @Bar2 bar) {}
201→                    if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
202→                        node.isFirstAnnotationEntry()
203→                    ) {
204→                        emit(startOfList.startOffset, "Expected newline after '${startOfList.text}'", true)
205→                        if (autoCorrect) {
206→                            startOfList
207→                                .upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(
208→                                    getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent).plus(indentConfig.indent),
209→                                )
210→                        }
211→                    }
212→                    if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() &&
213→                        node.isLastAnnotationEntry()
214→                    ) {
215→                        node
216→                            .findAnnotatedConstruct()
217→                            .treeParent
218→                            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
219→                            .nextLeaf { it.isCodeLeaf() && it.elementType != ElementType.COMMA }
220→                            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
221→                                emit(codeLeaf.startOffset, "Expected newline before '${codeLeaf.text}'", true)
222→                                if (autoCorrect) {
223→                                    codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
224→                                }
225→                            }
226→                    }
227→                }
228→        }
229→
230→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() && node.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine()) {
231→            // Code below is disallowed
232→            //   @Foo1
233→            //   @Foo2 @Foo3
234→            //   fun foo() {}
235→            emit(
236→                node.startOffset,
237→                "All annotations should either be on a single line or all annotations should be on a separate line",
238→                true,
239→            )
240→            if (autoCorrect) {
241→                node
242→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
243→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
244→            }
245→        }
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.typeProjectionOrNull() =
249→        takeIf { elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
250→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST }
251→            ?.treeParent
252→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE }
253→            ?.treeParent
254→            ?.takeIf { it.treeParent.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION }
255→            ?.treeParent
256→
257→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByAnnotationOnAnotherLine(): Boolean {
258→        val firstAnnotation = treeParent.findChildByType(ANNOTATION_ENTRY)
259→        return siblings(forward = false)
260→            .takeWhile { it != firstAnnotation }
261→            .any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
262→    }
263→
264→    private fun checkForAnnotationWithParameterToBePlacedOnSeparateLine(
265→        node: ASTNode,
266→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
267→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
268→    ) {
269→        if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry()) {
270→            emit(
271→                node.startOffset,
272→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
273→                true,
274→            )
275→            if (autoCorrect) {
276→                node
277→                    .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
278→                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
279→            }
280→        }
281→
282→        if (node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct()) {
283→            emit(
284→                node.startOffset,
285→                "Annotation with parameter(s) should be placed on a separate line prior to the annotated construct",
286→                // Annotated expressions for which the annotation contains a parameter can be hard to correct
287→                // automatically. See examples below. For now, let them be fixed manually.
288→                // fun foo1() = @Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()
289→                // if (@Suppress("DEPRECATION") bar()) { .. }
290→                node.treeParent.elementType != ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION,
291→            )
292→            if (autoCorrect) {
293→                node
294→                    .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
295→                    .nextLeaf()
296→                    .safeAs<LeafPsiElement>()
297→                    ?.let {
298→                        if (it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
299→                            it.replaceWithText(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
300→                        } else {
301→                            it.rawInsertBeforeMe(
302→                                PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent)),
303→                            )
304→                        }
305→                    }
306→            }
307→        }
308→    }
309→
310→    private fun ASTNode.isNotReceiverTargetAnnotation() = getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() != AnnotationUseSiteTarget.RECEIVER
311→
312→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget() =
313→        psi
314→            .safeAs<KtAnnotationEntry>()
315→            ?.useSiteTarget
316→            ?.getAnnotationUseSiteTarget()
317→
318→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotationEntryWithValueArgumentList() = getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() != null
319→
320→    private fun ASTNode.getAnnotationEntryValueArgumentList() =
321→        takeIf { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
322→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST)
323→
324→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstAnnotationEntry() =
325→        this ==
326→            treeParent
327→                .children()
328→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
329→
330→    private fun ASTNode.isLastAnnotationEntry() =
331→        this ==
332→            treeParent
333→                .children()
334→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
335→
336→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
337→        siblings(forward = false)
338→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
339→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
340→
341→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntryOnTheSameLine() =
342→        siblings()
343→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
344→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
345→
346→    private fun ASTNode.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() =
347→        siblings(forward = false)
348→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
349→
350→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry() =
351→        siblings(forward = false)
352→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
353→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
354→
355→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() = siblings(forward = true).any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
356→
357→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry() =
358→        siblings(forward = true)
359→            .takeWhile { it.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
360→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct(): Boolean {
363→        val annotatedConstruct = findAnnotatedConstruct()
364→        return lastChildLeafOrSelf()
365→            .leaves(forward = true)
366→            .takeWhile { it != annotatedConstruct }
367→            .none { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
368→    }
369→
370→    private fun ASTNode.findAnnotatedConstruct(): ASTNode {
371→        val astNode =
372→            if (treeParent.elementType == MODIFIER_LIST) {
373→                treeParent
374→            } else {
375→                this
376→            }
377→        return checkNotNull(
378→            astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextCodeLeaf(),
379→        )
380→    }
381→
382→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntryOrAnnotatedConstruct() =
383→        if (isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
384→            isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()
385→        } else {
386→            isOnSameLineAsAnnotatedConstruct()
387→        }
388→
389→    private fun visitFileAnnotationList(
390→        node: ASTNode,
391→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
392→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
393→    ) {
394→        node
395→            .lastChildLeafOrSelf()
396→            .nextCodeLeaf()
397→            ?.let { codeLeaf ->
398→                val whitespaceBefore = codeLeaf.prevLeaf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
399→
400→                if (whitespaceBefore == null || whitespaceBefore.text != "\n\n") {
401→                    emit(
402→                        codeLeaf.startOffset,
403→                        "File annotations should be separated from file contents with a blank line",
404→                        true,
405→                    )
406→                    if (autoCorrect) {
407→                        codeLeaf.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n\n")
408→                    }
409→                }
410→            }
411→    }
412→
413→    private fun visitAnnotation(
414→        node: ASTNode,
415→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
416→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
417→    ) {
418→        require(node.elementType == ANNOTATION)
419→
420→        if ((node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsNextAnnotationEntry()) ||
421→            (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry() && node.isOnSameLineAsPreviousAnnotationEntry())
422→        ) {
423→            emit(
424→                node.startOffset,
425→                "@[...] style annotations should be on a separate line from other annotations.",
426→                true,
427→            )
428→            if (autoCorrect) {
429→                if (node.isFollowedByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
430→                    node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
431→                } else if (node.isPrecededByOtherAnnotationEntry()) {
432→                    node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(getNewlineWithIndent(node.treeParent))
433→                }
434→            }
435→        }
436→    }
437→
438→    private fun getNewlineWithIndent(modifierListRoot: ASTNode): String {
439→        val nodeBeforeAnnotations =
440→            modifierListRoot
441→                .treeParent
442→                .treePrev
443→        // Make sure we only insert a single newline
444→        val indentWithoutNewline =
445→            nodeBeforeAnnotations
446→                ?.text
447→                .orEmpty()
448→                .substringAfterLast('\n')
449→        return "\n".plus(indentWithoutNewline)
450→    }
451→}

 127→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 128→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 129→                    ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,

 131→                ),
 132→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 133→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 134→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 135→                ),
 136→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 137→                    ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 138→                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 139→                ),
 140→            ),
 141→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 142→            setOf(
 143→                CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 144→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 145→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 146→            ),
 147→    ) {
 148→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 149→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 150→
 151→    private var line = 1
 152→
 153→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 154→
 155→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 156→
 157→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 158→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 159→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 160→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 161→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 162→        )
 163→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 164→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 165→        }
 166→    }
 167→
 168→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 169→        node: ASTNode,
 170→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 171→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 172→    ) {
 173→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 174→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 175→            node
 176→                .nextLeaf()
 177→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 178→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 179→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 180→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 181→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 182→                    }
 183→                }
 184→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 185→        }
 186→
 187→        when {
 188→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 189→                line++
 190→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 191→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 192→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 193→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 194→                    )
 195→                }
 196→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 197→            }
 198→
 199→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 200→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 201→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 202→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
 203→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 204→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 205→                startIndentContext(
 206→                    fromAstNode = node,
 207→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 208→                )
 209→
 210→            node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
 211→                visitValueArgument(node)
 212→
 213→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 214→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 215→
 216→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 217→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 218→                startIndentContext(node)
 219→
 220→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 221→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 222→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 223→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ||
 224→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE ->
 225→                startIndentContext(node)
 226→
 227→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 228→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 229→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 230→                startIndentContext(
 231→                    fromAstNode = node,
 232→                    childIndent = "",
 233→                )
 234→
 235→            node.elementType == IF ->
 236→                visitIf(node)
 237→
 238→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 239→                visitLbrace(node)
 240→
 241→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 242→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 243→                startIndentContext(
 244→                    fromAstNode = node,
 245→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 246→                )
 247→
 248→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 249→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 250→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 251→
 252→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 253→                visitValueParameter(node)
 254→
 255→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 256→                visitFun(node)
 257→
 258→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 259→                visitClass(node)
 260→
 261→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 262→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 263→
 264→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 265→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 266→
 267→            node.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION -> {
 268→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 269→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 270→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 271→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 272→                ) {
 273→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 274→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 275→                    //    .bar[0] {
 276→                    //        "foobar"
 277→                    //    }
 278→                    startIndentContext(
 279→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 280→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 281→                    )
 282→                } else if (node.treeParent.elementType in CHAINABLE_EXPRESSION) {
 283→                    // Multiple dot qualified expressions and/or safe expression on the same line should not increase the indent level
 284→                } else {
 285→                    startIndentContext(node)
 286→                }
 287→            }
 288→
 289→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 290→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 291→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 292→
 293→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 294→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 295→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 296→
 297→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 298→                visitWhen(node)
 299→
 300→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 301→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 302→
 303→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 304→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 305→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 306→
 307→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 308→                visitKdoc(node)
 309→
 310→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ||
 311→                node.elementType == TYPEALIAS ->
 312→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 313→
 314→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 315→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 316→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 317→
 318→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 319→                visitLBracket(node)
 320→
 321→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 322→                visitNullableType(node)
 323→
 324→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION ->
 325→                visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 326→
 327→            node.elementType == TRY ->
 328→                visitTryCatchFinally(node)
 329→
 330→            else -> {
 331→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 332→            }
 333→        }
 334→    }

 339→            //     val foo = foo(
 340→            //         parameterName =
 341→            //             "The quick brown fox "
 342→            //                .plus("jumps ")
 343→            //                .plus("over the lazy dog"),
 344→            //     )

 352→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 353→        node
 354→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 355→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 356→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 357→                val nextToAstNode =
 358→                    startIndentContext(
 359→                        fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 360→                    ).prevCodeLeaf()
 361→
 362→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 363→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 364→                    startIndentContext(
 365→                        fromAstNode = node,
 366→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 367→                        childIndent = "",
 368→                    )

 370→            }
